title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm going to have a rain check.
So maybe when I was younger, my dad would say, all right, I'm going to work.
So if I'm going to the bar with my girlfriends, maybe you'll say, have fun tonight, but no monkey business.

title: will / shall
examples: I'll say, I'm really sorry, I have plans already, but can I get a rain check?
Oh, you'll do great.
So if I'm going to the bar with my girlfriends, maybe you'll say, have fun tonight, but no monkey business.
Because it's a little bit of an older expression, I'll say.
They'll be funny if I'm using to your family.

title: will / won't
examples: I messaged her, but she won't reply.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: So you can say, I don't want to be a third wheel, or I felt like a third wheel.
So a rain check is when you can't attend something, or you have to decline an invitation, but you want to ask if you can do that later.
I'll say, I'm really sorry, I have plans already, but can I get a rain check?
Can we have a rain check?
Can I get a rain check?

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: So that person might feel a little bit awkward or uncomfortable or even less doubt.
When there's an extra person in a group, that might feel left out.

title: have to / must
examples: So a rain check is when you can't attend something, or you have to decline an invitation, but you want to ask if you can do that later.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: So would you say New York or Seoul, like living there can be a rat race?
So maybe when I was younger, my dad would say, all right, I'm going to work.
This one is, I would say, an older expression.
Yeah, it's something like my dad would say, maybe.
If you said this to my dad, I think he'd crack up.
